focus: auto-select + factory + registry provider precedence and fallback dimension handling

graphEvents.label: P1 non-Ollama active provider ignored by startup resolver

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Provider selection spans bootstrap persistence, factory resolution, startup validation, registry fallbacks, vector shard attachment, and save/cache consumers.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Prompt-selected EMB-1/provider precedence and fallback dimension path; targets were chosen along the bootstrap selected-provider flow, then the later factory/startup resolver and first consumers of pre-init model/dimension.

findingDetails.title: Startup resolver ignores persisted non-Ollama active embedders, so local-first bootstrap can be overwritten by stale cloud config
findingDetails.claim: ensureActiveEmbedder can persist hf-local, OpenAI, or Voyage from the local-first bootstrap cascade, but the later factory startup resolver only recognizes persisted Ollama metadata. If hf-local wins bootstrap while a stale usable VOYAGE_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY is present, startup validation and EMBEDDING_DIM can switch the process back to the cloud provider/dimension even though the active shard was attached for the persisted local profile.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether resolveProvider reads generic active metadata, whether getProviderInfo reuses the bootstrap metadata after non-Ollama persistence, and whether tests cover hf-local selected while a cloud key exists. The generic reader exists only for hf-local dimension drift warnings, getProviderInfo calls resolveProvider again, and tests cover hf-local auto-selection plus no-key Ollama recovery but not stale cloud env after non-Ollama persistence.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Operators may treat any usable cloud key as intentional. That does not fit the local-first bootstrap contract, because startup has already persisted and attached the selected active profile before the cloud validation/dimension path runs.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if startup is changed so resolveProvider consumes persisted active_embedder_provider for all provider kinds, or if EMBEDDING_DIM and pre-init model/dimension readers are proven unreachable after a non-Ollama active pointer is persisted.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The prior EMB-1 explicit-provider override issue may have a new, separately reportable branch.

searchActions.result: Startup still initializes and attaches active embedder before provider config validation.
Auto-select remains local-first while factory explicit provider resolution still gives EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER precedence.
Explicit provider override bootstrap is already registered as DR-001-P1-001 / EMB-1.

searchLedger.rationale: The explicit override branch remains active but is already captured; this iteration does not re-report it as new.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A non-Ollama active pointer persisted by local-first bootstrap can be ignored by the later factory resolver, causing provider/dimension drift under partial-provider availability.

searchActions.result: ensureActiveEmbedder persists whichever auto-selected provider was selected, including hf-local/openai/voyage.
resolveProvider only considers readActiveOllamaEmbedder; that path rejects non-Ollama names via getOllamaManifest, while the generic active metadata reader is not part of provider selection.
Startup attaches active shard before validation, then sets EMBEDDING_DIM from resolveStartupEmbeddingConfig/getStartupEmbeddingDimension; pre-init getEmbeddingDimension reads that factory path.
Save/cache path reads model/dimension before generating, so wrong pre-init dimension can affect profile/cache lookup.

searchLedger.rationale: The selected active provider is not a single source of truth across bootstrap, factory startup, and dimension consumers.

searchLedger.invariant: Factory fallbacks should report dimension drift when a created provider falls back to a different dimension.

searchActions.result: createFallbackProvider compares requestedDim with fallback provider metadata and records dimensionChanged metadata.
Custom hf-local dimensions are reported through the onDimensionResolved callback after first embed.
Tests cover unlisted hf-local runtime dimension adoption.

searchLedger.rationale: No new direct factory fallback dimension-warning defect found; the new issue is earlier selection-source drift, not missing fallback warning metadata.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Fallback creation and custom hf-local runtime dim paths both have drift reporting hooks.

searchLedger.invariant: Canonical provider fallback names and dimensions should derive from the shared registry or provider dimension maps without split constants drifting silently.

searchActions.result: getCanonicalFallback derives ollama and hf-local local names from MANIFESTS[0], and cloud names from CLOUD_CANONICAL.
Factory default models and valid dimensions derive from provider dimension maps and registry fallbacks.
Auto-select still carries fixed local/cloud dimensions for probe results, but current registered local defaults are 768 and cloud defaults match provider maps.

searchLedger.rationale: No new registry fallback drift found in the current one-manifest state; fixed auto-select dimensions remain a maintainability watchpoint but not a new defect in this pass.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Current fallback names/dimensions are consistent across registry, factory, and auto-select for the registered defaults.

title: Startup resolver ignores persisted non-Ollama active embedders, so local-first bootstrap can be overwritten by stale cloud config
claim: ensureActiveEmbedder can persist hf-local, OpenAI, or Voyage from the local-first bootstrap cascade, but the later factory startup resolver only recognizes persisted Ollama metadata. If hf-local wins bootstrap while a stale usable cloud API key is present, startup validation and EMBEDDING_DIM can switch the process back to the cloud provider/dimension even though the active shard was attached for the persisted local profile.
counterevidenceSought: Checked generic active metadata use in resolveProvider, cached provider-info reuse after non-Ollama bootstrap, and tests for hf-local bootstrap under stale cloud env; none covers or prevents this branch.
alternativeExplanation: A usable cloud key could be intentional, but startup has already selected, persisted, and attached a local active profile before the cloud path runs.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if resolveProvider is changed to honor persisted active_embedder_provider for all provider kinds, or if pre-init dimension/model readers cannot execute after non-Ollama active persistence.

reason: Explicit provider override bootstrap remains active and is not re-reported; this iteration found a distinct non-Ollama active metadata/dimension drift branch.

createFallbackProvider records dimensionChanged and hf-local custom dimensions report drift through onDimensionResolved; the new defect is source-of-truth drift before those fallback hooks, not absent fallback metadata.
